original: Total solar eclipse visible from North America
Walt Disney's animated movie "Bambi," based on the book byFelix Salten, is released
Voyager 2 begins a flyby of Neptune
Water ice is first detected on the Moon by India's Chandrayaan-1 spacecraft (2008-9) in findings published by scientists
The Mona Lisa is stolen from the Louvre Museum by Italian decorator Vincenzo Peruggia, recovered in 1913
Egyptian authorities unveil parts of a 2,000-year-old sunken city submerged off the coast of Alexandria, including buildings, statues, artifacts, and an ancient dock
Korean pop groupBTSreleases the single "Dynamite," the first video to be watched more than 100 million times in 24 hours on YouTube
Marvel superhero film "Blade," starringWesley Snipes, is released
Charles Martinet, the voice of Mario in Nintendo's video games, retires after 27 years
Hawaii becomes the 50th state of the United States
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthbecomes the first MLB player to hit 600 career home runs as NY defeats the St. Louis Browns 11-7 at Sportsman's Park
Canada's largest earthquake since 1700 strikes the Queen Charlotte Fault below Haida Gwaii at 8.1 magnitude, knocking cows off their feet
Future Danish astronomer 14-year-oldTycho Brahebecomes interested in astronomy after observing a solar eclipse
Masai Graham wins funniest joke at Edinburgh Fringe with "I tried to steal spaghetti from the shop, but the female guard saw me and I couldn't get pasta."
Nigeria goes three years without a case of polio in a landmark step toward the eradication of the disease
Striker Carli Lloyd scores in extra time as United States beats Brazil 1-0 to win the women'sfootball gold medalat Beijing Olympics
The Australian women's 4 x 100 m medley relay team of Giaan Rooney, Leisel Jones, Petria Thomas, and Jodie Henry smash the world record to take gold in 3:57.32 at the Athens Olympics

Kenyan runnerEliud Kipchogewins the Olympic men's marathon gold medal at the Rio de Janeiro Games in a time of 2:08:44; Feyisa Lilesa (Ethiopia) is second in 2:09:54, and Galen Rupp (US) is third in 2:10:05
Dutch swimmer and cancer survivor Maarten van der Weijden wins inaugural men's open water marathon (10 km) in 1:51:51.6 at the Beijing Olympics
Milwaukee Brewers relief pitcher Rollie Fingers becomes the first pitcher to achieve 300 saves
American golferScottie Schefflermakes birdies on four of the final five holes to become the 12th player in PGA Tour history to shoot a 59 in the second round of the Northern Trust at TPC Boston
Gemini V launched into orbit, carrying astronauts Gordon Cooper and Charles "Pete" Conrad

American pair Misty May-Treanor and Kerri Walsh dominate China's Tian Jia and Wang Jie 21-18, 21-18 in the beach volleyball final to repeat their gold medal from Athens; they don't lose a set in the competition
Kevin Durantscores 30 as the US retains the Olympic men's basketball title with a comfortable 96-66 win over Serbia at the Rio de Janeiro Games
Hurricane Dean, the first storm since Hurricane Andrew to make landfall as a Category 5, makes landfall in Costa Maya, Mexico, with winds at 165 mph
Ian Bothamtakes his world-record 356th Test cricket wicket against New Zealand at The Oval
"Dirty Dancing," a film directed by Emile Ardolino and starringPatrick Swayzeand Jennifer Grey, opens in the US
Russian Bolshoi Ballet dancer Alexander Godunov defects in New York City, joins the American Ballet Theatre, and works as a film actor

Nicolás Massú and Fernando González win the men's doubles tennis at the Athens Olympics, defeating the German pair Nicolas Kiefer and Rainer Schüttler in 5 sets; it is Chile's first Olympic gold medal

New Zealand clinches their 10th Tri Nations Rugby Series with one match remaining, scoring 2 tries in the last 3 minutes to beat South Africa 29–22 in the first-ever Test at FNB Stadium in Johannesburg

South Africa clinches its second Tri Nations Rugby Series on points differential with a 23-19 win over Australia in Durban; the Wallabies score 3 tries to 2, but Percy Montgomery lands 5 goals for the Springboks
London's Parliament clock, Big Ben, chimes for the last time before a four-year restoration process for its tower begins
74,155 fires caused by land clearing are burning in the Amazon rainforest, the most ever recorded, according to Brazil's National Institute for Space Research
American country singerPatsy Clinerecords struggling songwriter Willie Nelson's song "Crazy" in Nashville, Tennessee
A jury in Sheffield, Massachusetts, grants two slaves, Bett (later Elizabeth Freeman) and Bron, their freedom after Freeman brings the case to court

Attorney Samuel Tucker organizes a peaceful sit-in at the public library in Alexandria, Virginia, in an effort to grant Black patrons equal access; five young men are arrested for disorderly conduct. No trial is ever held, and charges are officially dropped 80 years later, and the community builds a separate facility nearby
US orbiting astronomy observatory Copernicus is launched
First hot air balloon flight over the Alps
PFC James Anderson Jr. is the first African American Marine to receive the Medal of Honor in the Vietnam War
Typhoon Fred ravages Chinese county Zhejiang, killing 700+
Latvia declares its independence from USSR
American inventor William Seward Burroughs patents the adding machine
Kublai Khanaccepts the surrender of his younger brother Ariq Böke at Xanadu, at the end of the Mongol civil war
A 1642 will at the center of a legal battle over Shakespeare's home is found after 150 years in an unlabeled box at the National Archives

The Beatles headline their second show of the day at Busch Memorial Stadium in St. Louis, Missouri, after playing in the afternoon 341 miles away in Cincinnati, Ohio, following a rainout the night before
Battle, East Sussex: Mary Langdon becomes the first British firewoman
110°F (43°C) recorded in Fayetteville, North Carolina (state record)
US government forces closure of Hudson Foods due to E. coli outbreak
The Red Cross announces that a famine is striking Tajikistan and calls for international financial aid for Tajikistan and Uzbekistan
Cleveland Indians 47-inning scoreless streak is broken as future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher Bob Lemon yields a home run to Aaron Robinson in a 3-2 loss to Chicago White Sox
NASA loses contact with Mars Observer
Surrey wicket-keeper Ted Pooley completes a first-class cricket record of eight stumpings in a county match against Kent at The Oval
The Virgin Mary, along with St. Joseph and St. John the Evangelist, reportedly appears to the people of Knock, County Mayo, Ireland
John Hampton patents the Venetian blind
Jarvis Island is discovered by the crew of the ship Eliza Frances
The Vienna Stadtpark, the first public park and garden in Vienna, Austria, opens its gates
Sea battle at Kijkduin: De Ruyter defeats English and French fleets
Friz Freleng, American animator for Warner Bros cartoons (Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies), born in Kansas City, Missouri (d. 1995)
Joe Strummer [Mellor], British punk rock lead singer and songwriter (The Clash - "Rock the Casbah";"Lost in the Supermarket"; "London Calling"), born in Ankara, Turkey (d. 2002)
(Marie-Juliette) "Lili" Boulanger, French composer (first female winner of the Prix de Rome composition prize), born in Paris (d. 1918)
